D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

The Abney Effect

slide white into a spectral hue and watch its perceived colour drift even as the dominant wavelength holds

Diluting a colour with white keeps its dominant wavelength fixed, yet its apparent hue shifts. So lines of constant perceived hue bow away from the straight purity lines.

The straight line is constant dominant wavelength as white is mixed in; the curved line is constant perceived hue. Slide purity to move the swatch along the straight path and watch its hue leave the curve.
